title: Biases in Electronic Health Records Data for Generating Real-World Evidence

dataset_schema.label: Administrative and Billing Data
dataset_schema.description: ICD codes, insurance type, billing records.

dataset_schema.label: Clinical and Medical Data
dataset_schema.description: Lab tests (LOINC), SNOMED/CPT codes, vital signs, medications, free-text notes, radiology images.

original_intent.label: Clinical
original_intent.description: Patient care, medical history documentation, clinical decision-making.

repurposed_use: Combined as real-world data to generate clinical evidence for research, treatment evaluation, and decision support outside controlled trials.

limitations.label: Selection Bias
limitations.description: Only includes individuals interacting with healthcare systems, excluding underserved populations.

limitations.label: Documentation Bias
limitations.description: Data reflects billing incentives rather than research completeness.

limitations.label: Information Bias
limitations.description: Incomplete histories and misclassification of diseases.

limitations.label: Temporal Instability
limitations.description: Recorded timestamps may not reflect true clinical events.

transformations.label: NLP Processing
transformations.description: Extract structured information from clinical notes.

transformations.label: Feature Engineering
transformations.description: Phenotyping using rules or machine learning.

notes: Highlights representational bias where data reflects healthcare system processes rather than underlying clinical reality. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
